mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for memory management and request batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources and potential complexity in setup for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface to interact with the service.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.7

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a managed Kubernetes cluster using Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install vLLM Helm Chart
steps.description:
Deploy vLLM on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.18.0 --namespace vllm --create-namespace

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the vLLM pods.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up any necessary data, then upgrade the vLLM deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.18.0 --namespace vllm

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Cordon and drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the latest version of vLLM.
> Verify that the pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.17.0, ensure to update your model configurations to align with new quantization methods.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
